articles.title: How Nanocoder's daemon replaces the scheduler
articles.focus: Explain the daemon's architecture: Unix socket IPC, lockfile lifecycle, debounced file watching, per-subscription concurrency caps, and how headless mode differs from the removed scheduler mode.

articles.title: Choosing your Nanocoder extension point
articles.focus: Map the three extension surfaces — custom commands (prompt injection), Custom Tools (shell-quoted markdown), and MCP servers (external process + full protocol) — and give concrete guidance on which to reach for and when.

articles.title: LLM-based compaction and its fallback chain
articles.focus: Describe how the summariser prompt produces a structured markdown summary (Context / Decisions / Files modified / Tools used / Open questions), when it falls back to mechanical truncation, and why the per-provider contextWindow override matters for the firing threshold.
